current_edit_state.concrete_change: The first real v2 locality edit is now checked in: compute_init stays on the accepted v1 bias-fused path, then the tile-local data_pad patch is staged one dc_0 16-channel reduction slice at a time and immediately reused across all three c_1 groups before the next slice is prepared.
preserved_non_changes: the accepted v1 bias-fused compute_init/compute_update path remains intact
data_pad remains materialized, but is now staged a dc_0 slice at a time instead of once per c_1 group
the scheduled tiling, reduction split dc_0 x dc_1 = 6 x 16, and pragma_auto_unroll_max_step = 32 remain unchanged
the branch is still local-only, diagnostic-only, and not hook-facing

notes: This working copy is derived from the accepted v1 scheduled-form baseline rather than from the raw reference seed alone.
The purpose of v2 is to isolate the first real transpose1-style locality branch without disturbing the accepted v1 baseline.
The checked-in v2 edit now stages the padded tile one dc_0 slice at a time for reuse across all three c_1 groups.
The new candidate remains local-only and diagnostic-only until the edited path is locally rebuilt and, if warranted, board-validated.
